About the job

Brief Description




tango is looking for a detail-oriented self-starter to work directly with our Network Operations team to provide administrative and data entry support in helping manage the configuration of internal software and processes within the Network and Credentialing departments. The Configuration Specialist is responsible for ensuring the tango provider network is accurate and adequate. Additionally, the Configuration Specialist will support audits and projects that affect agency contracting and credentialing. This is an entry level position that will provide the Configuration Specialist experience and skill development around data integrations, data governance, and data structure and reporting.




Responsibilities




Essential functions of the job are listed below. Other responsibilities may also be assigned. Please note that the essential functions may vary depending on department size, organizational structure and/or geographic location. Reasonable accommodations may be made to allow differently-abled individuals to perform the essential functions of the job.




Primary Responsibilities




Configure and maintain software applications and systems to meet organizational needs and requirements.
Create and update documentation for configuration processes, settings, and modifications.
Ensure compliance with internal policies, standards, and regulatory requirements.
Provide support for configuration-related issues, troubleshooting problems, and implementing solutions.
Provide intra-departmental information and support on basic to moderate in-house automated system configuration.
Creating and maintaining all Provider records and demographic information housed within internal software configuration systems.
Participating and contributing to internal committees or meetings that may involve, note-taking, problem-solving, implementation of change requests, etc.
Developing and writing job aides, and processes, and assisting in policies for configuration of internal platforms.
Communicate with direct Supervisor and Network team with any issues regarding providers, documents, or other information pertaining to managing the data in our software(s) and platforms.
Communicate process improvement opportunities and work inter-departmentally on process change alongside the direct Supervisor.
Assisting in problem-solving the configuration of different payment parameters for providers.
Communicating any changes to provider information and/or payment parameters downstream to applicable departments.
Creating or assisting in creating reports in tango’s internal software platforms applicable to Credentialing and provider Contracts.
Analyzing data from reports and summarizing findings and key metrics to direct supervisor.
Leading weekly or monthly team meetings and conducting knowledge transfer of key findings from reports and current Networks.
Meeting intra-departmentally with Claims and Accounting teams to improve efficiencies and workflows between departments.
Comply with HIPAA and tango policies.
Maintain privacy and confidentiality at all times.
Various other duties, in support of the Network function, on a daily and weekly basis as assigned by the direct Supervisor and as needed by the Credentialing and Network team(s).




Additional Responsibilities




This role is under the Director of Network Operations who also manages the Credentialing and Network Operations Department. It will involve working closely with additional Credentialing Specialists to meet the needs of the Network Department with focus on Configuration, Credentialing, and Network management.




Minimum Qualifications




The following are the minimum qualifications that an individual needs in order to successfully perform the duties and responsibilities of this position. Please note that the minimum qualifications may vary based upon the department size and/or geographic location.




Knowledge/ Experience




Equivalent to high school diploma.
Proficient use of Microsoft Office and Adobe applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).
Consistent and reliable WIFI.
Experience in healthcare (home health or credentialing a plus), or similar role, preferred but not required.




Skills/ Abilities Pertinent To This Position




Communication Skills: Strong written and verbal communication skills are essential for effectively communicating with providers, stakeholders, and internal teams. This includes the ability to draft formal communications, respond to inquiries promptly and professionally, and convey information clearly.
Attention to Detail: A strong eye for detail is important to ensure accuracy and completeness in managing provider data, documentation, and communication materials. Attention to detail is also crucial in troubleshooting issues and testing new features in the Provider Portal.
Collaboration: The ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams, including the direct Supervisor, Network team, and other departments, is vital for identifying process improvement opportunities and implementing changes. This involves strong interpersonal skills, flexibility, and the ability to work well in a team environment.
Confidentiality: Maintaining privacy and confidentiality is critical when dealing with sensitive provider information, ensuring compliance with data protection regulations and ethical standards.
Organizational Skills: Strong organizational skills are essential for managing multiple tasks, prioritizing responsibilities, and meeting deadlines. This includes the ability to handle email and voicemail correspondence promptly and efficiently.
Technical Aptitude: Proficiency in using and troubleshooting software applications, particularly in working with the Provider Portal and in-house automated systems. This may also involve the ability to develop and document job aides, processes, and policies related to system configuration.
Adaptability: The ability to adapt to changes in processes, technologies, and responsibilities is crucial in a dynamic work environment. This includes being open to learning new skills and embracing opportunities for professional growth.






















tango provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. tango will make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with known disabilities unless doing so would result in an undue hardship.

About the job

Operations Generalist

Part-Time Contractor | 10–15 hours per week

Remote




About Us

Hippie is a fast-growing beverage portfolio company building modern, functional brands with strong operational discipline behind the scenes. As we scale across DTC and wholesale channels, operational excellence and clean financial data are critical to our growth.

We are looking for a detail-oriented, systems-minded Operations Generalist to support customer experience, fulfillment visibility, accounts receivable collections, and financial data hygiene. This is a part-time contractor role ideal for someone who thrives in structured environments and enjoys keeping the backend of a business running smoothly.




Role Overview

The Operations Generalist will work closely with our Operations Manager to ensure our customer support workflows, fulfillment tracking, and financial systems remain accurate and efficient. This role requires strong follow-through, comfort working across systems, and the ability to proactively identify inconsistencies.




Key Responsibilities

Customer Support

 • Manage customer support inbox and ticket queue

 • Respond to customer inquiries with professionalism and brand-aligned tone

 • Escalate complex issues as needed

 • Track recurring issues and flag trends to leadership

 • Maintain clean documentation of customer interactions

Fulfillment Operations

 • Monitor daily order fulfillment and shipment status

 • Track delivery progress and proactively identify delays

 • Ensure accurate data flow from Shopify to our 3PL system

 • Investigate and resolve fulfillment discrepancies

 • Coordinate with 3PL partners on exceptions and claims

Collections and AR Support

 • Assist with past-due invoice follow-ups and escalation workflows

 • Maintain accurate documentation of payment status

 • Coordinate with sales and leadership on collections strategy

 • Help ensure clear visibility into AR aging

Financial Data Cleanliness

 • Support reconciliation between Shopify and QuickBooks Online

 • Assist in identifying and correcting transaction discrepancies

 • Ensure clean financial categorization and documentation

 • Support ongoing improvements in operational reporting accuracy




What We’re Looking For

 • 2+ years experience in operations, customer support, finance support, or similar role

 • Strong attention to detail and high accuracy standards

 • Experience with Shopify and QuickBooks Online preferred

 • Comfortable working with fulfillment systems and 3PL coordination

 • Clear written communication skills

 • Highly organized and process-oriented

 • Able to work independently and proactively flag issues




Nice to Have

 • Experience in CPG or DTC ecommerce

 • Familiarity with AR collections processes

 • Experience reconciling data

 • Experience working in fast-growing startup environments




Time Commitment

 • 10–15 hours per week

 • Flexible schedule with availability during standard business hours for coordination




Compensation

Hourly contractor rate ranging from $15-$20/hr based on experience. 




Why This Role Matters

This role sits at the intersection of CX, fulfillment, and finance. As we scale, operational precision becomes a competitive advantage. The right person in this role will directly impact customer satisfaction, cash flow clarity, and leadership visibility into the business.







… more

Requirements added by the job poster

• 1+ years of experience in Customer Service

About the job

About the Opportunity:




The organization is seeking a Support Analyst to provide application support by answering complex questions on product function and usage. The role involves investigating, managing, tracking, and closing client support issues related to the database and application functions. The analyst will contribute to a knowledge base and support user forums while serving as the primary support liaison between the company and customers. This position includes working closely with the Director of Financials Support and a collaborative team to deliver exceptional customer service and development support.




Responsibilities:




• Serve as frontline support liaison responding to product and technical inquiries via phone or electronic tickets

• Assess software configuration, set-up, and code to identify resolutions or escalate per guidelines

• Document detailed information in client tracking system and follow defined processes and objectives

• Review submitted issue database and proactively follow up to ensure satisfactory resolution

• Collaborate with team members to exchange knowledge through training and peer interaction

• Maintain current knowledge of applications and related technologies

• Understand API and web services technologies and functions

• Troubleshoot email technologies

• Present support webinars internally and to customers via the web

• Apply engagement delivery and software troubleshooting techniques focusing on first call resolution

• Interpret requirements and recommend client-focused solutions

• Demonstrate strong analytical, research, and problem-solving skills

• Effectively multi-task and prioritize work

• Exhibit exceptional attention to detail and quick concept comprehension

• Perform other duties as assigned by management




Requirements:




• Experience in application support and client issue resolution

• Proficiency with software configuration, code review, and troubleshooting

• Knowledge of APIs, web services, and email technologies

• Ability to deliver webinars and provide training

• Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and multitasking skills

• Strong attention to detail and ability to understand complex concepts quickly




Benefits & Perks:




• Fully remote work

• Equipment provided




Compensation:




• $20-$22 per hour




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

About the job

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Complaint Analyst in United States.

In this role, you will be responsible for managing and investigating product complaints to ensure compliance with regulatory standards and company quality procedures. You will act as a key liaison between customers, field representatives, and internal teams to gather accurate information, track complaint progress, and verify resolution before closure. This position requires meticulous attention to detail, strong problem-solving skills, and the ability to manage multiple investigations in a fast-paced environment. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including Quality, Engineering, Regulatory, and Customer Service to drive timely, thorough investigations. The role offers opportunities to influence quality improvements, support compliance initiatives, and contribute to a culture of continuous improvement in a remote-friendly work environment. Your work will have a direct impact on product safety, customer satisfaction, and organizational integrity.

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: GTM Operations Analyst
description:
RemoteHunter

GTM Operations Analyst

United States · 13 hours ago · 33 people clicked apply

Promoted by hirer · Responses managed off LinkedIn

Remote
Full-time
Apply
Saved
Use AI to assess how you fit

Get AI-powered advice on this job and more exclusive features with Premium. Try Premium for $0

Show match details

Tailor my resume

Help me stand out

About the job

About the Opportunity:




The organization provides digital product companies with a simplified payment infrastructure by acting as a Merchant of Record, eliminating payment fragmentation. It serves over 5000 software sellers in 245 territories globally and is backed by notable investors. The role is for a detail-oriented GTM Operations Analyst within the Revenue Operations team, focusing on Deal Desk and Commissions, supporting sales processes, contract administration, and compensation calculations, with a clear career path in Revenue Operations management.




Responsibilities:




• Assist with review, approval, and processing of standard sales contracts, quotes, and order forms ensuring compliance with policies and pricing guidelines

• Maintain accurate deal details in Salesforce CRM and related financial systems

• Update internal documentation for deal structures, pricing rules, and approval workflows

• Serve as first contact for sales team inquiries on contract status, pricing, and approval routing

• Support validation of sales data and KPIs for commission calculations

• Assist with monthly and quarterly commission calculations and report generation

• Generate and distribute compensation statements and reports to sales personnel

• Conduct ad-hoc reporting and analysis on GTM performance, sales cycle metrics, and pipeline health

• Support GTM Operations team with administrative tasks and data cleanup in Salesforce




Requirements:




• Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or related field, or 1-2 years in an administrative or data-focused role

• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel/Google Sheets (e.g., VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, data manipulation)

• High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy

• Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills

• Preferred: Familiarity with Salesforce or major CRM platforms

• Preferred: Basic understanding of sales cycle and business terminology

• Preferred: Experience in financial analysis, accounting, or compensation administration




Benefits & Perks:




• Attractive salaries and stock options

• Retirement plans

• Private healthcare

• Well-being initiatives

• Flexible work options including remote or hybrid work from stylish hubs

• Unlimited holidays and enhanced parental leave

• Annual learning fund and regular training opportunities




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.
